methodology: Statistical independence test on maintenance-outcomes.json. Classified 16 sessions (S384-S433) by filter layer failures (temporal/quality/knowledge/attention). Computed observed vs expected co-occurrence rates under independence assumption.
expect: If cascades are systemic, layer failure co-occurrence should exceed independence baseline (ratio >> 1.0). Prediction: ≥5 sessions with causal cascade evidence.
actual: 5/6 layer pairs show co-occurrence ratio near 1.0 (range 0.98-1.14), indicating statistical independence. Only knowledge→attention shows excess (ratio=1.60, observed=7 vs expected=4.4). Multi-layer failure sessions produce MORE lessons (4.6 vs 2.7 L/session), opposite of cascade prediction.
diff: CONTRADICTS f-flt3-cascade-s434.json verdict. The 5 cascades (C1-C5) are individual bug instances, not systemic architecture. Production causes maintenance drift, not reverse.

productivity_confound.direction: Production causes DUE drift, not cascade causes low production

limitation: Only 16/50 sessions have structured maintenance-outcomes records. Selection bias possible.
verdict: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED — cascades exist as bug-class (L-1007 C1-C5), but architecture does not systematically cascade (independence ratios ~1.0)
